answer:
### Overview of Absolute Reality and the World

These three Medieval Kawi Śaiva texts—*Vr̥haspatitattva*, *Gaṇapatitattva*, and *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*—address the relationship between absolute reality (paramārtha or viśeṣa, centered on Śiva in his highest forms) and the empirical world (saṃsāra, manifested through māyā and tattvas) through emanationist ontology, microcosm-macrocosm unity, and yogic realization. All posit a triadic absolute (Paramaśiva/Sadāśiva/Śiva-tattvas), with the world arising from māyā as an unconscious veiling principle that "stains" consciousness (cetana), binding the ātmā in cyclic birth via karma-vāsanās and guṇas. The absolute pervades the world (ūtaprota: immanent like oil in milk, transcendent like string through jewels), yet remains unstained. Liberation reverses this via jñāna and yoga, dissolving back to the niṣkala (formless) source.[^1][^2][^3]

Key similarities: 
- **Emanation**: World from māyā/pradhāna/triguṇa → buddhi/ahaṃkāra/manas/indriyas/tanmātras/mahabhūtas.
- **Pervasion**: Absolute fills world but ātmā forgets due to mala/vāsanā.
- **Path**: Ṣaḍaṅgayoga (six-limbed: pratyāhāra etc.), daśaśīla (ten virtues), burning vāsanās for siddhis/mokṣa.

Differences emerge in emphasis: *Vr̥haspatitattva* stresses karmic multiplicity and tattva hierarchy; *Gaṇapatitattva* tantric Oṃkāra-body unity; *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna* padas (states) and ātmā grades.

### *Vr̥haspatitattva*: Hierarchical Emanation and Karmic Binding

Absolute reality is twofold cetana (Paramaśivatattva: formless, infinite; Sadāśivatattva: active with śaktis/aṣṭaiśvarya; Śivatattva) pervading acetana māyā.[^4] World arises when Śivatattva stains with māyā-mala, producing pradhānatattva (forgetfulness), triguna (sattva-rajas-tamas citta causing births: mokṣa/heaven/human/animal/hell/sthāvara), buddhi (dharma/jñāna etc.), ahaṃkāra (vaikṛta/taijasa/bhūtādi → manas/indriyas/tanmātras/mahabhūtas). Diversity from vāsanās (latent karma, like asafoetida scent). Relationship: World is illusory projection (blind men/elephant simile); absolute utaprota (pervasive yet pure). Mokṣa via ṣaḍaṅgayoga, burning tattvas to Paramaśiva.[^5]

### *Gaṇapatitattva*: Tantric Emanation from Oṃkāra and Body-Cosmos Identity

Absolute from Oṃkāra → bindu → five selves (Brahmā/Viṣṇu/Rudra/Īśvara/Sadāśiva), subtle elements (scent/taste/form/touch/sound → mahabhūtas → cosmos/body). World/cosmos/human identical (puruṣa=viśva); selves in cakras (mūlādhāra=nābhi etc.).[ ^6] Māyā as primordial illusion; world recursive dissolution (unmanifest→nāda→bindu→moon→universe→Oṃ). Relationship: Non-dual unity—body is liṅga/temple, world dissolved in self-liṅga. Less saṃsāra emphasis; praxis via nyāsa/mantras/praṇava for śūnyanirvāṇa.[^7]

### *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*: Graded Ātmā and States of Pervasion

Absolute: Paramaśivatattva (niṣkala, beyond); Sadāśivatattva (sakala-niṣkala powers); Ātmikatattva (cetana stained by acetana māyā).[ ^8] World: Māyā→pradhāna→triguṇa→buddhi/ahaṃkāra→indriyas etc.; ātmā grades (pramāṇa/viśeṣa) via padas (jāgrat/svapna/suṣupti/tūrya/tūryānta). Upasargas (guṇa-obstacles) in yoga. Relationship: Absolute utaprota in all (crystal simile); world saṃsāra for bound ātmā (śakaṭopama: cart pulled by ātmā-ox, Īśa-charioteer). Mokṣa: Prayogasandhi (ṣaḍaṅga + saptāṅga/āmṛta/āgni) burns vāsanās to anantaviśeṣa.[^9]

### Comparative Analysis

| Aspect                  | *Vr̥haspatitattva*                          | *Gaṇapatitattva*                           | *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*                     |
|-------------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| **Absolute Structure** | Parama-/Sadā-/Śiva-tattvas (cetana)        | Oṃ→bindu→5 selves (Sadāśiva highest)       | Parama-/Sadā-/Ātmika-tattvas (cetana > māyā) |
| **World Origin**       | Māyā-mala stains Śiva → pradhāna→36 tattvas| Oṃkāra emanation; body=cosmos             | Māyā→pradhāna→guṇas→tattvas; padas bind ātmā |
| **Relation**           | Transcendent pervasion (ūtaprota); vāsanā-forget| Identity (body-liṅga=world); recursive diss.| Graded pervasion (crystal); ātmā states/grades |
| **Binding Principle**  | Karma-vāsanā, triguna-citta births         | Illusion (māyā); guṇas minor              | Upasargas, vāsanā of guṇas; ātmā descent    |
| **Liberation Means**   | Ṣaḍaṅga yoga + daśaśīla; burn tattvas      | Praṇava-nyāsa, ṣaḍaṅga; self-liṅga worship| Prayogasandhi (ṣaḍaṅga + saptas); siddhis  |

*Vr̥haspatitattva* most systematic (full tattva list, elephant partialism); *Gaṇapatitattva* practical-tantric (mantras/chakras); *Saṃ Hyaṃ* psychological (padas, upasargas). All affirm non-dual pervasion, world as contracted absolute for bound ātmā, yoga as reversal—echoing Śaiva Siddhānta/Trika synthesis.

[^1]: *Vr̥haspatitattva*, Ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/).
[^2]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, Ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/).
[^3]: *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*, Ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/).
[^4]: get_passage on Paramaśivatattva description: [p-8da993](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-8da993) to [p-6250fd](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-6250fd).
[^5]: Blind men/elephant: [p-2027be](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-2027be); utaprota: [p-500d5a](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-500d5a).
[^6]: Five selves/chakras: [p-d3e477](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-d3e477) to [p-5105ea](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-5105ea).
[^7]: Dissolution: [p-37a5c1](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-37a5c1).
[^8]: Triad + māyā: [p-daaeef](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-daaeef) to [p-34f807](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-34f807).
[^9]: Crystal simile: [p-f06b83](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-f06b83); prayogasandhi: [p-b51cad](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-b51cad).
